name: Repository Standards
description: Ten-gate repository-agnostic enforcement: architecture, performance, portability, security, testing, CI, documentation, API design, release readiness, truth. Zero dead code, zero drift, zero untested paths, low memory, low CPU.

phases.name: Architecture & Idioms
phases.principle: Is the structure sound, modular, and idiomatic?

delegates.task_template: Audit repository architecture for: ${goal}. Enforce Ports-and-Adapters: Core logic MUST NOT import I/O, UI, or infrastructure layers directly. Score Tech Debt (target 10/10) and Modularity (target 100/100). Verify dependency graph depth <=5, no circular dependencies, no god modules (>500 lines). Enforce root directory cleanliness: max 7 top-level items (src, docs, tests, config, README, LICENSE, build manifest). Consolidate scattered config files into config/ directory. Verify single source of truth for project metadata (one manifest file).

Enforce language idioms appropriate to this repository for: ${goal}. Detect the primary language and enforce its canonical idioms: naming conventions, import style, error handling patterns, modern language features, standard formatting. Flag: dead imports, shadowed variables, unused parameters, mutable state where immutable suffices, stringly-typed APIs, manual loops where higher-order functions apply, blocking calls in async context. Enforce consistent naming: file names, function names, type names, constants follow the language community standard.
verifies: language idioms enforced for primary language
no dead imports or unused parameters
no blocking calls in async context

delegates.task_template: Conduct deep research on current best practices for: ${goal}. Identify the repository's primary language and ecosystem. Research: latest recommended project layouts, current community standards for linting/formatting/testing, modern patterns replacing deprecated approaches, zero-cost architecture patterns that minimize memory and CPU. Cross-validate findings against at least 2 authoritative sources (official docs, core team recommendations). Flag any pattern in the codebase that contradicts current community consensus. Produce actionable recommendations with citations.

Audit file organization and directory structure for: ${goal}. Verify every source file belongs to a named module or directory with a clear purpose. Remove orphaned files not referenced by any build target or import. Enforce consistent file naming convention (detect dominant style and enforce it globally). Flag empty directories, temporary artifacts (.DS_Store, *.log, *.tmp, *.bak), and abandoned backup files. Verify test directories mirror source directories. Standardize import paths to match directory structure.
verifies: every file belongs to a named module
no temporary artifacts or backup files

phases.checkpoint: Architecture clean, idioms enforced, best practices researched, files organized
phases.failure_message: Architecture gate failed. Fix structure, idioms, and file organization before proceeding.

phases.name: Performance & Resources
phases.principle: Is every operation fast, lean, and resource-efficient?

delegates.task_template: Profile the codebase for performance and resource efficiency for: ${goal}. Identify hot paths and measure execution time, memory allocation, and CPU usage. Flag: unnecessary allocations, copies where references suffice, synchronous blocking in async contexts, O(n^2) or worse algorithms where O(n log n) is feasible, unbounded collection growth, missing caching for expensive pure computations. Verify startup time is within budget. Measure memory footprint under typical load: flag if resident memory exceeds 2x minimum required. Provide benchmarks as evidence for every optimization claim. Target: low memory, low CPU, zero waste.
verifies: no O(n^2) where O(n log n) is feasible

delegates.task_template: Audit dependency health and bloat for: ${goal}. Identify and remove unused dependencies. Replace heavy dependencies with lighter alternatives where possible. Flag excessive transitive dependency count. Verify all dependencies are actively maintained (release within 12 months). Check for duplicate functionality across dependencies. Verify lock file is committed and reproducible. Audit build scripts for unnecessary work, network access, or filesystem writes.
verifies: heavy deps replaced with lighter alternatives

phases.checkpoint: Performance profiled, resources optimized, dependencies lean
phases.failure_message: Performance gate failed. Unjustified resource usage or dependency bloat detected.

phases.name: Portability & Platform Parity
phases.principle: Does it build and pass identically on every platform?

delegates.task_template: Verify cross-platform portability for: ${goal}. Ensure identical behavior on macOS, Linux, and Windows. Flag platform-specific behavior leaks: path separators, line endings, locale-dependent operations, timezone handling, endianness assumptions, temp directory assumptions. Verify all platform-specific code is isolated behind explicit guards and tested. Test on all target platforms: verify no linker errors, no missing symbols, identical test results. Flag any hardcoded paths (use $HOME or environment variables). Verify no platform-specific tooling required for build or test.
verifies: builds on macOS, Linux, and Windows
tests pass identically on all platforms

phases.checkpoint: Cross-platform parity verified on macOS, Linux, and Windows
phases.failure_message: Portability gate failed. Platform-specific leaks or build failures detected.

phases.name: Safety & Security
phases.principle: Is the code safe and the supply chain clean?

delegates.task_template: Security audit of the codebase for: ${goal}. Scan for: injection vulnerabilities (SQL, command, path traversal), unsafe deserialization, hardcoded secrets or API keys, eval/exec on user input, insecure temporary file creation, commented-out code blocks >3 lines without dated TODO. Verify: all user input validated at system boundaries, all file operations use safe patterns, all subprocess/shell invocations use parameterized forms (no string interpolation). Flag severity and provide remediation for each finding. Run language-appropriate security scanner.

Audit supply chain security and define security policy for: ${goal}. Scan all dependencies for known CVEs. Check for unmaintained dependencies (>12 months without release). Verify license compatibility (flag copyleft in permissive projects). Flag typosquatting risks on package names. Verify lock file is committed and dependencies are pinned. Audit build hooks and post-install scripts for suspicious behavior. Classify threat level for each finding using S0-S3 severity. Provide security clearance or block with specific requirements.
verifies: no known CVEs in dependency tree

phases.checkpoint: Code security verified, supply chain audited, no vulnerabilities
phases.failure_message: Safety gate failed. Security vulnerabilities or supply chain risks detected.

phases.name: Testing & Coverage
phases.principle: Is every code path tested with evidence?

delegates.task_template: Enforce comprehensive test coverage for: ${goal}. Achieve 100% line coverage and 90%+ branch coverage. Generate property-based tests for core logic: serialization roundtrips, parser invariants, mathematical properties. Parametrize edge cases: empty inputs, nil/null/None values, Unicode strings, boundary values, concurrent access. Verify mock discipline: external services (HTTP, databases, filesystems) MUST be mocked in unit tests. Enforce: no test interdependency (tests pass in random order), no sleep()/delay() in tests, deterministic fixtures. Run mutation testing to verify test effectiveness. Add regression tests for every bug fix.

phases.checkpoint: 100% coverage, property tests, edge cases, mocking discipline, mutation tested
phases.failure_message: Testing gate failed. Coverage gaps or missing test categories detected.

phases.name: CI & Automation
phases.principle: Does CI enforce every standard automatically?

delegates.task_template: Enforce CI pipeline for: ${goal}. Configure: linter with zero-warning policy (deny all warnings), formatter check (fail on unformatted code), test suite with coverage threshold (fail-under 100%), type checker in strict mode (if applicable), security scanner in pipeline, dependency audit in pipeline. Set warnings-as-errors globally. CI matrix MUST include macOS and Linux at minimum. Cache dependencies for faster builds. Set timeout on test jobs (max 10 minutes). Add nightly job for: dependency update checks, beta/nightly compiler testing, extended test runs. Verify CI blocks merge on any failure. No '|| true' on security-critical steps.
verifies: CI matrix includes macOS and Linux

phases.checkpoint: CI pipeline enforces all standards, zero tolerance for violations
phases.failure_message: CI gate failed. Pipeline gaps or unenforced standards detected.

phases.name: Documentation
phases.principle: Is every public API documented with runnable examples?

delegates.task_template: Audit and perfect all documentation for: ${goal}. Every public function, class, type, and module MUST have a doc comment with: one-line summary, parameter descriptions, return description, error/exception conditions, at least one runnable example. README MUST include: one-sentence description, installation command, quickstart example that runs, configuration reference, platform requirements, license. Verify all code examples in docs execute correctly. Build documentation site without warnings. Structure: Getting Started, User Guide, API Reference.
verifies: Getting Started / User Guide / API Reference structure

delegates.task_template: Audit all documentation and user-facing content for brand voice compliance for: ${goal}. Enforce the Euxis Standard: authoritative, concise, imperative mood. Cut all hedging ('you might', 'it seems', 'this should'), filler ('basically', 'simply', 'just'), passive voice, and corporate jargon ('leverage', 'utilize', 'robust', 'facilitate'). Verify: short declarative sentences (8-15 words), benefit before mechanism, active voice always, one idea per sentence. Audit README, guides, error messages, CLI help text, and doc comments. Output word count reduction and principle violations found.
verifies: brand voice audit complete with metrics

phases.checkpoint: All public APIs documented, examples verified, brand voice enforced
phases.failure_message: Documentation gate failed. Missing docs, broken examples, or voice violations detected.

phases.name: API Design & UX
phases.principle: Is the API ergonomic, discoverable, and user-friendly?

delegates.task_template: Audit API ergonomics and user experience for: ${goal}. Verify: error messages are actionable (state what went wrong, why, and how to fix), output respects NO_COLOR environment variable, exit codes follow convention (0=success, 1=error, 2=usage), diagnostic output goes to stderr, data output goes to stdout. If CLI: verify --help is comprehensive with examples, progress bars for long operations. If library: verify error types carry actionable context, public types implement standard display/debug traits, default values reduce boilerplate, generic constraints are minimal. Verify consistent naming follows language API design guidelines. Flag any 'Error occurred' without context.

phases.checkpoint: API ergonomic, errors actionable, UX polished
phases.failure_message: API design gate failed. Poor error messages, inconsistent naming, or UX issues detected.

phases.name: Hardening & Release
phases.principle: Is it release-ready with baselines, changelogs, and semver?

delegates.task_template: Stress test and establish performance baselines for: ${goal}. Benchmark all hot paths with language-appropriate tools. Set performance budgets: define acceptable p50/p95/p99 latencies. Test with large inputs: 10x and 100x typical workload. Profile memory under sustained load: verify no unbounded growth, no leaks. Verify build time is acceptable. Check output artifact size: strip debug symbols, verify no debug-only code leaks into production. Target: low memory, low CPU under sustained load.
verifies: no unbounded memory growth under load
low memory and CPU under sustained load

delegates.task_template: Perform exhaustive regression analysis for: ${goal}. Verify CI catches: performance regressions (benchmark comparison), coverage drops (fail if coverage decreases), new linter warnings, new compiler warnings on latest/nightly toolchain. Classify every change category and run language-aware pattern analysis (ownership, type safety, concurrency, resource management). Scan for deprecated tags or comments older than 6 months and plan removal. Verify dead code branches are unreachable and remove them. Check for dev dependencies leaked into production. Verify CI blocks regression merges.

Verify release readiness for: ${goal}. Execute pre-release checklist: all tests pass with zero failures, coverage at 100%, linting clean, no known vulnerabilities, documentation current. Determine correct semantic version bump (MAJOR for breaking, MINOR for features, PATCH for fixes). Verify version number consistent across all manifests. Generate changelog from commit history following Keep a Changelog format. Verify upgrade/migration instructions for breaking changes. Draft release notes with highlights. Verify rollback plan exists. Coordinate security clearance with sentinel.

phases.checkpoint: Stress tested, regressions blocked, release-ready with semver and changelog
phases.failure_message: Hardening findings detected. Review stress tests, regressions, and release readiness.

phases.name: Final Truth Gate
phases.principle: Is every claim backed by evidence?

delegates.task_template: Final gate for: ${goal}. Validate evidence from all prior phases. Verify: architecture scores at target (Tech Debt 10/10, Modularity 100/100), language idioms enforced, best practices researched with citations, performance profiled with evidence, cross-platform parity confirmed (macOS + Linux at minimum), security scans clean with S0-S3 classification, 100% line coverage achieved, CI pipeline enforces all gates, documentation complete with verified examples, brand voice audit passed, API design reviewed, stress tests passed, regressions blocked, release readiness confirmed. Cross-check agent findings for contradictions. Flag any unverified assertion. Issue PASS or FAIL verdict with evidence.
verifies: all gate claims backed by evidence

delegates.task_template: Compliance audit for: ${goal}. Scan for PII exposure in logs, error messages, API responses, and data stores. Audit all dependency licenses for compatibility with the project's license model (reject copyleft in permissive projects without approval). Verify copyright headers are present and correct across all source files. Check for unauthorized data flows to third-party endpoints. Verify .gitignore excludes sensitive files (.env, credentials, private keys). Flag any compliance violation with regulatory reference (GDPR, CCPA) and remediation. Issue PASS, FAIL, or WARN verdict.
verifies: no PII in logs or error output
copyright headers present on all source files

delegates.task_template: Adversarial review of all gate findings for: ${goal}. Challenge assumptions from every prior phase. For each gate's PASS claim: invert the assumption and test what happens if it is wrong. Perform pre-mortem: assume the release failed 6 months from now, enumerate the top 5 reasons why. Assess blast radius of remaining risks. Evaluate reversibility of all decisions made during the playbook run. Identify single points of failure. If all agents agree on a PASS, verify it is not groupthink by testing counter-scenarios. Issue PROCEED, PROCEED WITH SAFEGUARDS, or RECONSIDER verdict with confidence level.
verifies: blast radius assessed for remaining risks

phases.checkpoint: Reviewer sign-off, compliance cleared, critic challenge passed
phases.failure_message: Final truth gate failed. Unverified claims, compliance issues, or critic objections detected.
